verdict.answer_first: A states the core principle immediately: "The whole point of incremental is that **TS and JS coexist in the same build the entire time**..."
verdict.decisiveness: A gives a single, prescriptive path: "Here's the battle-tested plan." B introduces a major decision point mid-stream: "ts-migrate... Good if you want everything as .ts fast; skip it if you'd rather convert deliberately."
verdict.fabrication: A invents a repo name: "this one (`fable-profile`) looks like an eval project"
verdict.actionability: A provides a complete, copy-pasteable `tsconfig.json` and `npm` command, letting me start work in seconds. B only describes the settings, requiring me to write the config myself.
